# `ls` filters and JSON output.

exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name c1 -- echo a
exec eon add --cron '@daily'  --name c2 -- echo b
exec eon add --at  '+1h'      --name o1 -- echo c

# No filter → all three jobs present (verified by name; IDs are random).
exec eon ls --json
stdout '"name": "c1"'
stdout '"name": "c2"'
stdout '"name": "o1"'

# Kind cron.
exec eon ls --kind cron --json
stdout '"name": "c1"'
stdout '"name": "c2"'
! stdout '"name": "o1"'

# Kind oneshot.
exec eon ls --kind oneshot --json
stdout '"name": "o1"'
! stdout '"name": "c1"'

# Status enabled = all 3 at this point.
exec eon ls --status enabled --json
stdout '"name": "c1"'
stdout '"name": "c2"'
stdout '"name": "o1"'

# After disable, status enabled drops to 2.
exec eon disable c2
exec eon ls --status enabled --json
! stdout '"name": "c2"'

# Invalid filter values → exit 2.
! exec eon ls --kind weird
! exec eon ls --status weird
